Iran says shipping in the Strait of Hormuz stopped after Israel violated the ceasefire with strikes on Lebanon. IRGC said vessel traffic halted minutes after the attacks, while the White House maintains Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Ceasefire in Lebanon IS part of agreement, Israel is taking the world hostage — Seyed Mohammad Marandi, Tehran Uni Prof. He says war in Iran is a major defeat for Trump and Netanyahu, showing 'limitations of empire'.

Oil companies are quietly pushing back against Trump’s Iran peace plan because it could allow Iran to charge tolls on ships using the Strait of Hormuz. Executives warn that adding fees of around $2–2.5 million per shipment, along with higher insurance costs, would drive up global oil prices and could even expose companies to sanctions violations. They’ve been raising these concerns with officials like Marco Rubio and JD Vance, though the administration appears open to considering Iran’s proposal. Source: POLITICO
